data.body:
PASS 1 review — [T3] Integration feasibility (overlay). VERDICT: PASS / no findings.

This story integrates an INTERNAL, already-used Jira integration; it asserts no new external capability that T3 must verify-before-commit.

AFFIRMATIONS (tool-grounded):
- (a) technical_feasibility + (b) named-method/endpoint existence — MATCH. The FakeAcliClient mirrors and the live capture script (scripts/capture_jira_fixtures.py) drives the four enrichment methods that already exist in production: get_comment_map (src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/acli_graph.py:289), get_issuelinks_map (acli_graph.py:391), get_parent_map (acli_graph.py:189), search_issues. The endpoint POST /rest/api/3/search/jql is live in production (acli_graph.py:236/337/431). The fixtures flow through the real production fetch_snapshot enrichment (fetcher.py:298 constructs the real AcliClient via the documented _load_acli swap seam) — not a parallel path.
- (c) auth/HTTPS preconditions STATED + grounded. The live capture is HTTPS Basic auth over jira_url with env-only api_token (acli_rest.py:103/128), and is explicitly live-gated per AC3 ("scripts/capture_jira_fixtures.py captures + scrubs from a live Jira (gated)"). Secret-scrubbing on capture (AC1: no tokens/emails/accountIds in committed fixtures) is stated.
- (d) No critical capability gap here — the default suite is hermetic (fixtures served through the in-process fake), with the only live touchpoint (capture) correctly gated; no SPIKE is required for this story.

No T3 finding for this story. (The one externally-new capability in the epic — optional Jira-Cloud OpenAPI validation — lives in sibling 59ef and is tracked there.)
